title: Fix: Customer form submissions not appearing in admin dashboard immediately after submission

requirements.summary: Invalidate and refetch admin order list automatically when a customer submits a service order form
acceptanceCriteria: When a customer submits a service form, the new order appears in the admin dashboard without any manual refresh.
The admin dashboard order list is automatically re-fetched/invalidated after a new order is created.
No stale cache prevents the new order from being visible to the admin immediately after submission.

file_operations.description: After successfully submitting an order via the `submitOrderMutation`, invalidate the `allOrders` query key using `queryClient.invalidateQueries(['allOrders'])`. This ensures that any admin dashboard viewing the orders list will have its cache invalidated and will refetch the latest data, making the newly submitted order immediately visible to admins without requiring a manual refresh.

requirements.summary: Implement periodic auto-refresh of the admin orders list at regular intervals to display new submissions in near real-time
acceptanceCriteria: The admin dashboard periodically refetches the orders list automatically while the admin is on the dashboard screen.
New orders submitted by customers appear in the admin view within the polling interval without any manual action.

file_operations.description: Update the `useGetAllOrders` hook to enable React Query's `refetchInterval` option set to 15000 milliseconds (15 seconds). This will cause the query to automatically refetch the orders list every 15 seconds when the admin dashboard is active, ensuring new customer submissions appear in near real-time without requiring any manual refresh action by the admin.

Ensure the `useGetAllOrders` hook is used with the updated polling configuration. Optionally display a subtle indicator (e.g., a timestamp or sync icon) showing when the orders list was last refreshed, providing feedback to the admin that the data is being kept up-to-date automatically.
